page: Microsoft Azure F32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled, but the instance is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments. There are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing and gzip/bzip3 single-threaded operations placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are also strong, ranking in the top 25%. Database operations and memory access speeds are highly efficient, as demonstrated by top-tier Passmark database and memory scores. For machine learning, the server achieves top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference, though performance drops to average for large-scale 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F32as_v7 offers a high-density compute profile with dedicated physical cores, making it a viable option for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled from compute resources. This server is well-suited for CPU-intensive workloads, web serving, database operations, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F32as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage compatibility for remote disk configurations. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, high-speed single-threaded decompression, and excellent database operations throughput. Multi-core processing and memory bandwidth also rank in the top 25% of tested systems. Given its dedicated core allocation and high clock speed, the F32as_v7 is highly efficient for workloads requiring sustained compute power without hyperthreading. It is an optimal fit for database hosting, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32as_v7 is a 32-core AMD EPYC virtual machine with 128 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and database throughput, making it ideal for compute-heavy and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32as_v7 features a 32-core AMD EPYC CPU and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC processor with balanced memory, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-intensive and database workloads.
